name: Implement a binary search tree
task: Create /tmp/eval_bst/bst.py implementing a BST class with three methods: insert(value), find(value) returning True/False, and in_order() returning a list of all values in sorted order. The class should accept any number of integer inserts and handle duplicates by ignoring them.

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_bst/bst.py'); m = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(m); t = m.BST(); [t.insert(v) for v in [5,3,8,1,4,7,9,3]]; assert t.in_order() == [1,3,4,5,7,8,9], f'in_order got {t.in_order()}'; assert t.find(7) is True; assert t.find(2) is False; print('OK')
